zh.lifeSummary: 當Nicola de la Haye於1151年出生在英國英格蘭林可爾史爾時，她的父親Richard de la Haye28歲，母親Matilda de Vernon Lady of Varenguebec32歲。她於大約1174年與Gerard de Camville在英國斯塔福爾克里夫頓 霍爾結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1230年11月20日在英國林可爾史爾享年79歲，埋葬在英國林可爾史爾斯瓦頓。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Nicola de la Haye于1151年出生在英国英格兰林可爾史爾时，她的父亲Richard de la Haye28岁，母亲Matilda de Vernon Lady of Varenguebec32岁。她于大约1174年与Gerard de Camville在英国斯塔福爾克里夫頓 霍爾结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和2个女儿的父母。她去世于1230年11月20日在英国林可爾史爾享年79岁，埋葬在英国林可爾史爾斯瓦頓。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: la
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Hung, Cuong, Anh, Thanh, Ha, Hoang, Tuan, Chau, Hao, Hien, Hoa, Hue, Nam, Phong, Sinh, Tuong, Min, Tam, Tanh, Thai, Tham, Tinh, Yeong.  Chinese Hong, Hang, Sang, Tong, Chan, Chi, Hwa Ja, Hyung, Kam, Ki Bong, Kin, Man.
Vietnamese: from the Chinese surname 羅, see   Luo   1.
Vietnamese (Lã): from the Chinese surname 呂, see Lu 6 and 3 below.

variants.name: Luo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Jian, Yi, Ping, Yong, Zhen, Feng, Hong, Ming, Jin, Bin, Chen, Hai, Lan, Min, Huan, Hung, Shen, Tian.
Chinese:

Mandarin form of the surname 羅, meaning ‘net for catching birds’ or ‘to collect’ in Chinese: (i) from Luo (羅), the name of an ancient state (located in present-day Hubei province). After it was annexed by the state of Chu during the early Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ), people from the state migrated southwards and adopted Luo (羅) as their surname. (ii) from the Po Duo Luo (破多羅) and Chi Luo (叱羅) families from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China, who changed their original surnames to 羅 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) from the personal name Hu Se Luo (斛瑟羅), leader of an ancient Turkic tribe who are believed to have migrated to China during the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ). His descendants inherited his name Hu Se Luo (斛瑟羅) as their surname and shortened it to 羅 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iv) from the name of one of the seven families from the Bandun Man, an ethnic group residing in ancient southwestern China (located mainly in present-day Sichuan province).

nameMeanings.name: Haye
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Hans, Helmut, Willi.

North German, East Frisian, and Dutch: from a personal name, Frisian Hajo, ancient Germanic Haio; or from a short form of the female name Hadewig, composed of the elements hadu ‘battle, strife’ + wīg ‘war’.
German: variant of   Hey   4, 5.

variants.name: Deshayes
meanings: French: topographic name for someone who lived by an enclosure, from the plural of Old French haye ‘hedge’ (see Haye and Hayes ), with fused preposition and definite article des ‘from the’; or a habitational name denoting someone from Les Hayes, the name of several places in various parts of France. Compare Deshaies .

variants.name: Hayenga
meanings: East Frisian: patronymic from the personal name Hajo (see Haye 1). It is formed with the suffix -enga, a variant of -inga (from the patronymic suffix -ing and genitive plural ending -a).

variants.name: Hey
meanings: English (Yorkshire and Lancashire): variant of   Hay  .
Dutch: variant, mostly Americanized (or archaic) and Flemish, of Heij, a topographic name for someone who lived on a heath, Dutch hei, heide.
German: metonymic occupational name for a grower or mower of grass, from Middle High German höu ‘grass, hay’.

variants.name: Hayen
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Alphonse.  German Heinz, Wilhelm.
Flemish, East Frisian, and North German: patronymic from   Haye   1.

nameMeanings.name: Nicola
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: feminine form of   Nicholas .

variants.name: Nicholas
meanings: form of the post-classical Greek personal name Nikolaos, derived from nikē ‘victory’ + laos ‘people’. The spelling with -ch- first occurred as early as the 12th century, and became firmly established at the time of the Reformation, although Nicolas is still occasionally found. St Nicholas was a 4th-century bishop of Myra in Lycia, about whom virtually nothing factual is known, although a vast body of legend grew up around him, and he became the patron saint of Greece and of Russia, as well as of children, sailors, merchants, and pawnbrokers. His feast day is 6 December , and among the many roles which legend has assigned to him is that of bringer of Christmas presents, in the guise of ‘Santa Claus’ (an alteration of the Dutch form of his name, Sinterklaas).

variants.name: Nicolò
meanings: form of the post-classical Greek personal name Nikolaos, derived from nikē ‘victory’ + laos ‘people’. The spelling with -ch- first occurred as early as the 12th century, and became firmly established at the time of the Reformation, although Nicolas is still occasionally found. St Nicholas was a 4th-century bishop of Myra in Lycia, about whom virtually nothing factual is known, although a vast body of legend grew up around him, and he became the patron saint of Greece and of Russia, as well as of children, sailors, merchants, and pawnbrokers. His feast day is 6 December , and among the many roles which legend has assigned to him is that of bringer of Christmas presents, in the guise of ‘Santa Claus’ (an alteration of the Dutch form of his name, Sinterklaas).

variants.name: Niccolò
meanings: form of the post-classical Greek personal name Nikolaos, derived from nikē ‘victory’ + laos ‘people’. The spelling with -ch- first occurred as early as the 12th century, and became firmly established at the time of the Reformation, although Nicolas is still occasionally found. St Nicholas was a 4th-century bishop of Myra in Lycia, about whom virtually nothing factual is known, although a vast body of legend grew up around him, and he became the patron saint of Greece and of Russia, as well as of children, sailors, merchants, and pawnbrokers. His feast day is 6 December , and among the many roles which legend has assigned to him is that of bringer of Christmas presents, in the guise of ‘Santa Claus’ (an alteration of the Dutch form of his name, Sinterklaas).

historicEvents.title: Magna Carta
historicEvents.description: King John of England agreed to a charter of rights called the Magna Carta. The document was originally drafted by the Archbishop of Canterbury to establish peace between King John and a group of rebel barons. The Magna Carta was completed in 1215 and is now considered to be one of the greatest constitutional documents of all time.

historicEvents.title: France Declares War on England
historicEvents.description: France declared war on England as Louis VIII of France attacked Poitou and northern Gascony.

en.lifeSummary: When Nicola de la Haye was born in 1151, in Lincolnshire, England, United Kingdom, her father, Richard de la Haye, was 28 and her mother, Matilda de Vernon Lady of Varenguebec, was 32. She married Gerard de Camville about 1174, in Clifton Hall, Staffordshire, England. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 2 daughters. She died on 20 November 1230, in Lincolnshire, England, at the age of 79, and was buried in Swaton, Lincolnshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Nicola de la Haye est née en 1151, à Lincolnshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Richard de la Haye, avait 28 ans et sa mère, Matilda de Vernon Lady of Varenguebec, avait 32 ans. Elle a épousé Gerard de Camville environ 1174, à Clifton Hall, Staffordshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 2 filles. Elle est décédée le 20 novembre 1230, à Lincolnshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 79 ans, et a été enterrée à Swaton, Lincolnshire, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Nicola de la Haye 1151 in Lincolnshire, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Richard de la Haye 28 und ihre Mutter Matilda de Vernon Lady of Varenguebec 32. Sie hat um 1174 in Clifton Hall, Staffordshire, England Gerard de Camville geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Sie starb am 20. November 1230 im Alter von 79 Jahren in Lincolnshire, England und wurde in Swaton, Lincolnshire, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Nicola de la Haye è nata il nel 1151 nel Lincolnshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, sua padre, Richard de la Haye, aveva 28 anni e sua madre, Matilda de Vernon Lady of Varenguebec, aveva 32 anni. Lei sposato Gerard de Camville circa 1174, nel Clifton Hall, Staffordshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli e 2 figlie. Morì il 20 novembre 1230, nel Lincolnshire, England, all'età di 79 anni, e fu sepolta a Swaton, Lincolnshire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Nicola de la Hayeが1151年にイギリスイギリスリンコルンシュアイアで生まれた時，父Richard de la Hayeは28歳，母Matilda de Vernon Lady of Varenguebecは32歳であった。約1174年にイギリススタフォードシュアイアクリフトン ハルでGerard de Camvilleと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼女は1230年11月20日にイギリスリンコルンシュアイアで79歳で死去し，イギリスリンコルンシュアイアスワトンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Nicola de la Haye(이)가 1151년 영국 잉글랜드 링코실르에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Richard de la Haye(은)는 28세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Matilda de Vernon Lady of Varenguebec(은)는 32세였다. 그녀는 대략 1174년 영국 스태퍼드실르 클리프턴 홀에서 Gerard de Camville(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1230년 11월 20일 영국 링코실르에서 79세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 링코실르 스왓에 묻혔다.

mn.Boylestone, Derbyshire, England, United: Boylestone, Deorbyscir, Англи, Нэгдсэн Вант Улс

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Nicola de la Haye nasceu em 1151, em Lincolnshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, Richard de la Haye, tinha 28 anos e sua mãe, Matilda de Vernon Lady of Varenguebec, tinha 32 anos. Ela casou-se com Gerard de Camville aproximadamente 1174, em Clifton Hall, Staffordshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 2 filhas. Ela faleceu em 20 de novembro de 1230, em Lincolnshire, Inglaterra, com 79 anos, e foi sepultada em Swaton, Lincolnshire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Nicola de la Haye родилась в 1151 г. в местности линколншир, Англия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени Richard de la Haye было 28 лет, а матери, которую звали Matilda de Vernon Lady of Varenguebec, 32. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Gerard de Camville около 1174 г. в местности клифтон холл, стаффордшир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 2 дочери. Она умерла 20 ноября 1230 г. в местности линколншир, Англия в возрасте 79 лет и была похоронена в местности сватон, линколншир, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Nicola de la Haye nació en 1151, en Lincolnshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, Richard de la Haye, tenía 28 años y su mamá, Matilda de Vernon Lady of Varenguebec, tenía 32 años. Se casó con Gerard de Camville aproximadamente 1174, en Clifton Hall, Staffordshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos y 2 hijas. Murió el 20 de noviembre de 1230, en Lincolnshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 79 años, y fue sepultada en Swaton, Lincolnshire, Inglaterra.
